chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Misc. Appln. No. S – 226 of 2023 Cr. Bail Appln. No. S – 100 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case For hearing of main case 27.05.2024 Mr. Qudratullah Rajput, Advocate for Applicant in Cr.Misc. Appln. No.S-226/2023 and for Complainant in Cr. Bail Appln. No.S- 100/2024 Mr. Ghulam Murtaza Buriro, Advocate for Applicants in Criminal Bail Appln. No.S- 100/2024 Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Jatoi, Additional PG for the State Zubair Ahmed Shaikh, SSP, Khairpur, Imdad Ahmed Solangi, DSP/CIA, Inspector Muslim, Shaikh, SHO P.S B-Section Khairpur and Inspector Muhammad Faheem Naeem Abro O R D E R Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J;- SSP, Khairpur is present and submits that investigation is vigorously being pursued to find out missing girl and the clues so far found by him and his team show that applicant, and his family is also involved in disappearance of the girl. And as per some reports, yet to be verified, missing girl was taken away by his brother, namely Abdul Hameed last time and thereafter she has disappeared, probably done to death. He further submits that Abdul Hameed, brother of missing girl and Papoo her maternal-uncle, both have fled to kacha area to avoid investigation and are not traceable for the time being but they are pursuing them. He seeks further time of one month to resolve this case and submit a definitive report about whereabouts of missing girl and the fact whether she is alive or not, and if alive, she is where. In such context, learned counsel for applicants in Crl. Bail Appln. No.S-100 of 2024 has pleaded for grant of bail to both applicants, particularly Applicant No.1, namely Abdul Majeed, on

Bail Appln. No.S-100 of 2024 has pleaded for grant of bail to both applicants, particularly Applicant No.1, namely Abdul Majeed, on Page 2 of 2 the ground that his name does not appear in FIR and given by complainant in his further statement only. Learned Additional PG and SSP, Khairpur have no objection to grant of bail to Applicant No.1, namely Abdul Majeed and submit that name of Applicant No.2, namely Mukhtiar Ali is mentioned in FIR with a specific role and unless current investigation being conducted under the orders of this Court is complete, he may not be granted bail as he is still required. When confronted with the same, learned counsel for the applicant does not press his bail application for the time being. In view of above, Criminal Bail Application No.S- 100 of 2024 to the extent of applicant No.1, namely Abdul Majeed is allowed and he is granted bail subject his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) with P.R bond to the satisfaction of trial Court. However, the bail application of Applicant No.2, namely Mukhtiar Ali is dismissed as not pressed. After the current investigation is over and report is submitted, Applicant No.2 may repeat his bail application before the trial Court, which shall take an independent view of its previous orders in this connection. Cr. Misc. Appln. No.S– 226 of 2023 is adjourned to 01.07.2024 whereas Cr. Bail Appln. No.S-100 of 2024 is disposed of accordingly. Office to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matter. Judge ARBROHI
